#[derive(Debug)]
enum ParseError {
    InvalidFormat { message: String },
    OutOfRange { message: String },
    MissingField { message: String },
}

fn parse_coordinate(input: &str) -> Result<(f64, f64), ParseError> {
    // BAD: format! allocates error strings on every call, even success path
    let parts: Vec<&str> = input.split(',').collect();
    if parts.len() != 2 {
        return Err(ParseError::InvalidFormat {
            message: format!("expected 'x,y', got '{}'", input),
        });
    }
    let x: f64 = parts[0].parse().map_err(|_| ParseError::InvalidFormat {
        message: format!("'{}' is not a float", parts[0]),
    })?;
    let y: f64 = parts[1].parse().map_err(|_| ParseError::InvalidFormat {
        message: format!("'{}' is not a float", parts[1]),
    })?;
    Ok((x, y))
}
